comment: OTM-parity + beyond-OTM feature matrix. Only the judge flips `passes`. See LOOP-CHARTER.md §3-4 and research/00-product-pillars-v3.md. v1.2.0: re-opened 8 features after Roham's 14:40Z visual review found honest-empty-state was passing where data-rendering was needed.

features.title: Filterable Moments grid (the OTM centerpiece)
features.acceptance: As a trader, I can land on /moments, filter by Player + Tier + max-Price + Listed-only, sort by listing price ascending, and click into a specific moment in under 10 seconds. The table renders at least 50 rows and shows player, edition, serial, tier, listing price, set, series.
features.data_source: topshot.moments LEFT JOIN topshot.editions e ON m.edition_id = e.edition_id
features.implementation_hint: Server component with searchParams-driven filters; nuqs for URL state; TanStack table for sortable columns; pagination 50/page; existing components: Card, TierChip, Num, EmptyState
features.comparable_primary: OTM Moments filterable grid

features.title: Moment detail price chart with working 1D/7D/1M/3M/YTD/ALL tabs

fail_reasons.shape: Judge passed against flowId 47863705 which has 0 sales for that specific serial → chart was honestly empty → judge accepted empty state. Need to pick a flowId with ≥5 sales in the underlying moment (not serial-specific) to demonstrate the chart actually redraws.

features.acceptance: As a trader, I open a moment detail page for a flowId WITH AT LEAST 5 RECORDED SALES, click between time-window tabs (1D, 7D, 1M, 3M, YTD, ALL), and the chart line actually redraws with sale dots. The 1Y window shows multiple dots; the 1D window shows the most recent sale(s) or honest empty for old moments. Tab state survives a page refresh. The default time window on landing is 30D (not 24H — most low-volume moments have no 24h activity).
features.data_source: topshot.transactions WHERE moment_id = $id AND completed_at >= $window_start
features.implementation_hint: Existing MomentPriceHistory component — handover says tabs decorative; check if URL updates only or actually refetches; client-side state with prefetched data per window
features.comparable_primary: OTM moment detail price chart (1D/7D/1M/3M/YTD/ALL tabs)

features.title: Moment detail circulation breakdown (Owned / Listings / Locked / In Pack / Locker Room / Burned)

fail_reasons.shape: Need to verify the circulation breakdown actually shows non-zero buckets — honest empty state is NOT an acceptable PASS here. Judge must pick a flowId from an actively-traded edition.

features.acceptance: As a trader, I see for this edition: absolute count + % of total for each of Owned, Listings, Owned-locked, In a Pack, Locker Room, Burned. Numbers sum to total circulation. The numbers MUST be non-zero for at least Owned and Listings on a flowId with active marketplace presence. Default time window: 30D for any time-keyed metric on this page.
features.data_source: topshot.moments aggregated by edition_id, status flags
features.implementation_hint: Aggregate query over topshot.moments grouped by moment_status; handle moment_status='LISTED' empty by deriving from listing_price_usd IS NOT NULL
features.comparable_primary: OTM circulation block (Owned/Listings/Locked/InPack/LockerRoom/Burned)

features.title: Market Players view with MARKET CAP leaderboard column

fail_reasons.shape: Default window 24H caused most rows to show '—' for the delta column per Roham. Reshipping with 30D default. Also: Total Minted and Circ % only populating for some players — Researcher needs to investigate the JOIN.

features.acceptance: As a trader, I land on /players and see a sortable table ranked by MARKET CAP. Columns: # / Player / Team / # Editions / Total Minted / Circ % / Market Cap / 30D Δ% (DEFAULT 30D, NOT 24H — low-volume moments have no 24h activity for most rows). Sort headers: clickable, persistent across navigation. Total Minted + Circ % populated for ≥80% of visible rows (not just top players).
features.data_source: topshot.mv_player_market_cap (already exists per handover) JOIN topshot.players
features.implementation_hint: Read mv_player_market_cap, JOIN players for names/teams; TanStack table sortable; default sort: market_cap DESC
features.comparable_primary: OTM Players view with MARKET CAP column + 24h Δ%

features.title: CSV export from the Moments grid

fail_reasons.shape: Duplicate menu bars appearing on /moments per Roham 2026-05-17 14:40Z. Likely a header component nested inside a layout that already renders the same header. Builder needs to grep for the menu/toolbar component and ensure single mount point.

features.acceptance: As a trader, I click EXPORT on the /moments grid and a CSV downloads with the rows currently visible under my filters. The CSV opens cleanly in Excel/Numbers/Sheets. There is EXACTLY ONE menu bar / toolbar visible on the page — no duplicated EXPORT buttons or duplicated filter controls.
features.data_source: Same query as moments-grid, server action streaming CSV
features.implementation_hint: Server action returning text/csv; respect the current filter state from URL; cap at 10K rows with a clear UI message if exceeded
features.comparable_primary: LiveToken CSV export (Account Lookup)

features.title: Collector portfolio BAG table (currently empty despite N moments)

fail_reasons.shape: CONFIRMED BROKEN: /u/roham shows BAG SIZE 3000 + $15.5M listed + 485 players in header, but the BAG table below is EMPTY (zero rows). Header KPIs and Composition sidebar work; the central table population is broken. Likely a flow_address resolution mismatch or a SELECT clause issue in the bag query. Judge accepted because the header populated and the table headers exist — passed without asserting row count > 0.

features.acceptance: As a trader, I visit /u/roham (or any /u/<known-username>) and see the BAG TABLE POPULATED with one row per moment owned (≥100 rows for active collectors). At minimum: play, edition, serial, tier, last sale, current listing price, acquired-at. The header KPIs (BAG SIZE, EST. LISTED VALUE, P&L, DISTINCT PLAYERS) MUST match the table row count (BAG SIZE = row count).
features.data_source: topshot.moments WHERE owner_flow_address = (SELECT flow_address FROM users WHERE username = $u)
features.implementation_hint: Handover Section 2E says this is broken — root cause likely a flow_address resolution or schema mismatch. Read PortfolioBagTable.tsx + debug the query.
features.comparable_primary: OTM Collector portfolio (BAG table)

features.title: Set completion histogram (X/56 users at completion-count Y)

fail_reasons.shape: CONFIRMED BROKEN: Judge picked 'Rookie Debut - Series 2023-24' which has NO data in mv_set_completion_distribution. Page shows 'No completion data — Backfill may still be running' and 'No editions resolved'. Honest absence was accepted as PASS, but Roham wants the histogram to ship — meaning either the MV needs backfill OR the journey needs to pick a set that HAS MV data.

features.acceptance: As a trader, I open a set detail page for a POPULATED set (one with mv_set_completion_distribution rows) and see a bar chart of users-at-each-completion-level. The chart MUST have at least 3 visible bars. The journey selects from sets with actual MV data; if none exist, the loop fails-fast pending MV backfill (not honest-empty-PASS).
features.data_source: topshot.mv_set_completion_distribution
features.implementation_hint: MV exists per handover; need a bar chart component, Recharts BarChart sufficient
features.comparable_primary: OTM Set Completion bar chart (X/56 users at Y level)

features.title: Moment detail price-bucket sale histogram

fail_reasons.shape: Histogram failed visually — judge accepted empty SVG as PASS. Need data-bearing flowId + assertion that bars have non-zero height.

features.acceptance: As a trader, on a moment detail page for a flowId with ≥10 recent sales, I see a histogram BELOW the price chart bucketing recent sales by price band ($X, $Y, …) over the active window. The bars MUST render with visible heights (not empty SVG). Default window: 30D.
features.data_source: topshot.transactions GROUP BY price_bucket
features.implementation_hint: Recharts BarChart; bucket size auto-derived from price range
features.comparable_primary: OTM moment detail price-bucket sale histogram (below chart)

features.title: Search True Value by serial overlay

fail_reasons.shape: Need to verify serial input + overlay actually produces a price estimate (not empty) on a data-bearing edition.

features.acceptance: As a trader, on a moment detail page for an edition with broad sale history, I type a serial number into the 'True Value by serial' input and see a price estimate for that specific serial WITH a confidence band and comp-count footnote. If the model genuinely has no comps for that serial band, an honest 'not enough comps' message appears WITH the band-range it tried.
features.data_source: valuation engine over topshot.transactions, segmented by serial bands
features.implementation_hint: Use the existing TunedValuationOverlay component; wire serial input; if model coverage gap, show honest absence per voice doctrine
features.comparable_primary: OTM 'Search True Value by serial' input + chart overlay

features.title: Full Players directory with filters (League / Team / Active / Owned)
features.acceptance: As a trader, beyond the market cap leaderboard, I can filter the player directory by league (NBA/WNBA), team (multi-select), active vs retired, with persistent left-rail filters.
features.data_source: topshot.players + mv_player_market_cap
features.implementation_hint: Extension of players-marketcap with filter rail
features.comparable_primary: OTM Players directory with persistent left filter rail

features.title: Sets directory with floor + 7d volume per set
features.acceptance: As a trader, I browse, search, and filter every Top Shot set by series, release date, current floor, and 7-day volume.
features.data_source: topshot.sets JOIN topshot.editions + transaction aggregates
features.implementation_hint: Currently stub per handover; needs new page + sortable table
features.comparable_primary: OTM Sets directory with floor + 7d volume

features.title: Pack open tracker with What's Left / Listings / Price History

fail_reasons.shape: Roham's full scope per 2026-05-17 14:40Z: original price ✓, # packs ✓, but MISSING from current ship: lowest-ask per contained edition, # listed per edition, and the 'hits remaining' estimate. Re-open with widened scope.

features.acceptance: As a trader, I open a pack detail page (e.g. /packs/2026-nba-playoffs-standard) and see: (a) original USD price, (b) total packs minted, (c) NUMBER OPENED, (d) NUMBER UNOPENED (sealed), (e) % opened, (f) PACK CONTENTS table showing each edition with: player, play, tier, minted-per-edition, MOMENTS-IN-PACK count, LOWEST ASK currently on marketplace for each contained edition, NUMBER LISTED on marketplace per edition. Plus a 'Hits inside' badge per edition flagging Rare/Legendary/Ultimate parallels with the count remaining unopened (estimated as total_minted_in_pack × % unopened).
features.data_source: topshot.packs JOIN topshot.drops
features.implementation_hint: topshot.packs table exists per handover; UI is missing
features.comparable_primary: OTM Packs tracker (dropped-on, % opened, contents grid)

features.title: Player detail with editions matrix grouped by set/tier
features.acceptance: As a trader, I open a player's page and see an editions matrix: rows by set, columns by tier (C/R/L/F), with floor + market cap per cell; plus market cap rank; plus career volume table with 24h/7d/30d/1y/ALL.
features.data_source: topshot.editions WHERE player_id = $id + mv_player_*
features.implementation_hint: Handover Section 2D says bare header; needs matrix + rank + volume table
features.comparable_primary: OTM Player page (editions matrix grouped by set/tier)

features.title: Sniper alerts — listings below comparable-serial floor
features.acceptance: As a trader, I see a continuously-refreshing list of moments listed >2σ below the comparable-serial floor, with $ delta and time-on-market visible, click-to-buy linkout to nbatopshot.com.
features.data_source: topshot.moments WHERE listing_price_usd < (floor for comparable serial band)
features.implementation_hint: OTM Sniper was THE most-loved feature per research/00-foundation-v2.md §7; ship transparent rules engine, not opaque model
features.comparable_primary: OTM Sniper (the most-loved feature in the dead-tool canon)

features.title: User watchlist + price alerts (auth-protected)
features.blocked_reason: Requires user_state.* tables + Supabase Auth wiring; user_state schema reserved per handover but not yet populated
features.acceptance: As a logged-in trader, I add a moment or edition to my watchlist, set a price-alert threshold, and receive a notification when triggered.
features.data_source: user_state.watchlists + Supabase Realtime
features.implementation_hint: Auth-protected route; user_state.* schema exists in stub
features.comparable_primary: NFTGo watchlist with price alerts

features.title: Real-time activity feed (largest sales, big listings, big buys)
features.acceptance: As a trader, I land on /feed and see a ticking, auto-updating stream of recent transactions (≥$100), with player thumbnail, price, buyer, seller, edition, all clickable.
features.data_source: topshot.transactions ORDER BY completed_at DESC, Supabase Realtime subscription
features.implementation_hint: Handover says /feed is 'minimal' — needs auto-refresh + filtering
features.comparable_primary: Nansen smart-money feeds + OTM Sales Feed

features.title: On This Day archive (historic plays + retrospective)
features.acceptance: As a trader, I land on /on-this-day and see today-in-Top-Shot-history: notable plays from this calendar date, their current floors, and momentum.
features.data_source: topshot.plays JOIN topshot.editions WHERE date_of_play matches today
features.implementation_hint: Editorial layer — pure data, no model required
features.comparable_primary: OTM 5-Days-of-Locking dashboard / GitHub contribution-graph variant

features.title: Anomaly detection (wash trades, volume spikes, suspicious activity)
features.acceptance: As a trader, I see a feed of flagged anomalies (back-to-back buy/sell same wallet, sudden volume spike on a low-liquidity edition, listing-burn ratio diverging) with rules-engine transparency.
features.data_source: topshot.transactions windowed analysis
features.implementation_hint: Beyond OTM; ship transparent rules first, optional ML later
features.comparable_primary: Hildobby wash-trade Dune dashboard

features.title: IPFS CID provenance per moment (the credibility moat OTM never had)
features.acceptance: As a trader, on every moment detail page I see the IPFS CID for the video + image with a verify-on-chain link to FlowScan.
features.data_source: topshot.moments.image_urls / video_urls + on-chain CID lookup
features.implementation_hint: Per handover: 'IPFS CID provenance shipped' on-chain — surface it client-side
features.comparable_primary: OpenSea blockchain-details strip + Etherscan verified-contract badge

features.title: Cross-collector comparison (compare two portfolios side by side)
features.acceptance: As a trader, I enter two usernames and see their portfolios overlaid: shared moments highlighted, unique moments listed, P&L delta over 30d/90d/1y.
features.data_source: topshot.moments grouped by owner_flow_address
features.implementation_hint: Beyond OTM; useful for influencer-tracking + portfolio diffing
features.comparable_primary: OTM compare-collectors (deferred there) + Card Ladder Pro portfolio diff

features.title: Packs: Best Hits Remaining scanner (which sealed packs have the best unrevealed hits)
features.acceptance: As a trader, I open /packs/scanner and see a ranked list of currently-purchasable pack drops, sorted by Expected Value of remaining unopened packs. EV = SUM over each contained edition: (probability of hit per pack × current market value of that edition). I see for each pack: total sealed remaining, EV per pack, current pack purchase price (or marketplace price for resold sealed packs), and EV/price ratio. The list highlights packs where EV/price > 1.0 (positive EV buys).
features.data_source: topshot.packs JOIN topshot.pack_contents JOIN current marketplace floor per edition; opened_count / sealed_count from pack metadata; conditional probability from pack composition
features.implementation_hint: Per Roham: 'the most important thing people want to see on a pack page: which packs have the best hits still hidden in them that I can go out and buy.' This is the centerpiece pack-page feature. May need an MV that joins pack_contents to current edition floors. Reuse the packs-tracker page shape with EV calculation overlay.
features.comparable_primary: OTM Pack tracker (deferred there)
comparable_cross_domain: Magic: The Gathering booster-box EV trackers

features.title: Homepage 'what's new in this build' strip + nav callout for new directories
features.acceptance: As a trader, when I land on the homepage I see a horizontal strip near the top showing the last 3-5 features that shipped (pulled from progress.md 'Completed' section), with a one-line description and a link to the relevant route. The strip is dismissible (sets a localStorage flag for 24h). The main nav also surfaces the new directories (Sets, Packs, Collectors) if they aren't already in the nav.
features.data_source: progress.md parsed at build-time + features.json for the route mapping
features.implementation_hint: Per Roham: home page isn't surfacing what's new, so traders land on the same Market dashboard and don't notice the new features one click away. Lightweight build-time read of progress.md tail.
features.comparable_primary: Linear's 'what's new' changelog strip + product-update modals
comparable_cross_domain: GitHub release notes strip on repo page

features.title: META: change default time window from 24H to 30D across every time-tab control
features.acceptance: As a trader, on every page that has a time-window selector (1D/7D/30D/90D/1Y/ALL or similar), the DEFAULT selected window on landing is 30D — not 24H. The URL contains ?h=30d or equivalent. The data populates meaningfully for the 30D window (since 24H is too sparse for low-volume moments).
features.data_source: n/a — UI default change across every time-tab component
features.implementation_hint: Per Roham 2026-05-17 14:40Z: '24 hours is not a short enough window or not a long enough window. That's no problem. Let's just default to showing 30-day windows.' Find every TimeTabs component / window state init and change the default. Likely lives in components/primitives or a shared hook.
features.comparable_primary: TradingView default chart window (1D for active stocks; longer for sparse)

judge_quality_review_2026_05_17: Honest-empty-state was passing as PASS on viz features where data rendering was the substance. Re-opened with explicit non-zero-bars / non-zero-rows assertions and data-bearing entity selection. Default time window 24H → 30D across every time-tab page.
